title: Module 5 knowledge check
markdown: Strong vs. weak prompts, hallucination, and when not to use AI.

questions.prompt: Which prompt is stronger for a useful draft?
choices: Draft a 120-word customer email announcing a Friday loyalty discount; friendly tone; do not invent features; one clear call to action.
Write something good.
Be creative about stuff.
Make it viral.

questions.prompt: Hallucination in generative AI usually means:
choices: Confident-sounding output that is false or unsupported
The model running out of electricity
A user typing too slowly
Guaranteed correct citations

questions.prompt: A good verification habit is to:
choices: Check important claims against a reliable source before acting
Always trust fluent text
Never iterate a prompt
Paste confidential HR files into any chatbot

questions.prompt: You should paste confidential personal data into third-party AI tools whenever it might save time.
choices: True
False

questions.prompt: When is AI often a poor fit without expert review?
choices: High-stakes decisions that require guaranteed correctness
Brainstorming informal blog title ideas you will edit
Rephrasing a public paragraph for clarity
Listing synonyms for a common word
Deliberately revising the prompt and comparing results
Submitting the first output to a court as evidence
Training a new foundation model overnight
Deleting all constraints
